title: AI Programming Paradigm Shift - December 2025 Turning Point
description: Andrej Karpathy announced that AI programming underwent a fundamental change around December 2025. Before December: coding agents were 'basically useless.' After December: models showed significantly improved quality with better long-term consistency and resilience, capable of completing large, time-consuming tasks. This marks enough quality to 'disrupt traditional programming workflows.' Karpathy shared an example where he gave an AI agent instructions to log into his DGX Spark, set up SSH keys, install vLLM, download and test Qwen3-VL, create a video inference server endpoint, build a basic Web UI dashboard, and configure services with systemd.
author: Andrej Karpathy

title: "The Hottest New Programming Language is English" - Karpathy
description: Andrej Karpathy declared that 'The hottest new programming language is English,' emphasizing how natural language has become the primary interface for programming through AI agents. David Heinemeier Hansson (Ruby on Rails creator) agreed, calling it the 'biggest, fastest change' in his 40 years with computers.
author: Andrej Karpathy

title: Qwen3.5-35B-A3B: Agentic Coding 'Game Changer' - Completes 5-Hour Programming Task
description: February 26, 2026: Reddit's r/LocalLLaMA discusses Qwen3.5-35B-A3B as a 'game changer for agentic coding.' Model successfully completed a 5-hour programming interview question, demonstrating capabilities for running on secondary graphics cards. Community impressed by its ability to handle complex, multi-step coding tasks without requiring high-end hardware.
author: Reddit Community

title: Gemini 3.1 Pro Release - Karpathy: 'The Age of App Stores is Over'
description: February 19, 2026: Gemini 3.1 Pro released with Tsinghua University's Yao Shunyu endorsement. Andrej Karpathy commented: 'the age of app stores is over,' discussing 'vibe coding' and creating custom tools in 1 hour. Discussion highlights shift toward AI-native CLI tools and APIs rather than traditional frontend interfaces. Community explores implications for software distribution and monetization.
author: Andrej Karpathy, Community
